triageFlags.title: Excessive Loan-to-Value Ratio
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio significantly exceeds the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application as the LTV ratio is non-compliant with policy.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the maximum LTV for Tier C applicants is 95%. The current LTV of 224.44% is far above this limit, indicating an unsustainable loan structure.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Threshold
triageFlags.description: The Debt-to-Income ratio exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application due to affordability concerns.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum DTI of 35% for Tier C applicants. The current DTI of 55% indicates that the applicant's financial obligations are unsustainable relative to their income.

content.reasoning: The analysis identified critical violations in Loan-to-Value and Debt-to-Income ratios, which are fundamental to the underwriting policy. These issues indicate a high risk of default.
